message:
🐛 Fix console flickering caused by non-composited animations (#496)

* 🐛 Fix console flickering caused by non-composited animations

Root cause: Agent status rapidly toggling between connected/disconnected
triggers card-refresh-pulse animation repeatedly, causing massive CLS.

Fixes applied:
- Changed loading spinners from border-b-2 to border-2 border-transparent
  border-t-primary pattern for GPU acceleration during rotation
- Changed card-refresh-pulse animation from border-width/box-shadow
  (non-compositable) to opacity (GPU-acceleratable)
- Added will-change hints for spin animations in Tailwind config
- Disabled animation trigger for offline state (forceSkeletonForOffline)
  to prevent flicker when agent status changes
- Added translateZ(0) to custom spin keyframes to force GPU compositing
- Added hysteresis to agent connection status: require 2 consecutive
  successes to reconnect after disconnection (prevents rapid flicker)

Performance impact:
- Eliminates TRANSFORM_RELATED_PROPERTY_CANNOT_BE_ACCELERATED_ON_TARGET
- Reduces layout shifts from non-composited animations
- Prevents card flicker during agent reconnection
- Stabilizes offline badge visibility

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Reduce forced reflows in scroll position tracking

Performance trace revealed 130ms of forced reflows from useLastRoute.ts
due to getBoundingClientRect() being called on every card in loops.

Optimizations:
- Limit card iteration to first 12 cards when saving scroll position
- Find target card by title text first (cheap) before measuring (expensive)
- Increase scroll debounce from 1s to 2s to reduce reflow frequency

This should reduce UI flickering caused by expensive layout calculations
during scroll events and navigation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Fix unreachable clusters showing as healthy

When the agent reports a cluster as unreachable (reachable: false), the UI
was still showing it as "Healthy" due to two bugs:

1. In shared.ts: clearClusterFailure() was called on ANY response, even if
   that response said the cluster was unreachable. This reset the 5-minute
   failure counter, so clusters never reached the offline threshold.

2. The code waited 5 minutes before showing offline status even when the
   agent explicitly reported reachable: false. The agent has direct access
   to clusters and its assessment should be trusted immediately.

Fixes:
- Only clear failure tracking when cluster is actually reachable
- Trust agent's reachable: false flag immediately without waiting 5 minutes
- Simplify useClusterHealth hook to show unreachable status right away

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Fix unreachable clusters losing OpenShift distribution

- Add URL-based distribution detection for unreachable clusters
- OpenShift clusters are identified by server URL patterns
  (*.openshiftapps.com, :6443 API servers, IBM FMAAS pattern)
- Fix merge logic to use || pattern preserving existing distribution
- Ensures platform-eval and vllm-d show as OpenShift even when offline

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
